1964:def _fetch_content(
1965:    url: str,
1966:    max_chars: int,
1967:    doi_hint: str = "",
1968:    pmid_hint: str = "",
1969:) -> tuple[str, bool, str, str, str]:
1970:    """Fetch URL content using the AccessBypass cascade (Crawl4AI +
1971:    Jina Reader + Firecrawl concurrent, fallback to direct HTTP +
1972:    Archive.org + institutional proxy + Sci-Hub).
1973:
1974:    Returns (content, ok, title, body_type, jsonld). Diff-gate P1-C: `jsonld`
1975:    is the raw ld+json <script> block contents extracted from the RAW fetched
1976:    HTML BEFORE _strip_html removes <script> blocks, so the structural junk
1977:    classifier (Signal C: NewsArticle / press-release / login-wall) can fire on
1978:    the live ON path. Empty when the winning backend already returned cleaned
1979:    text (Jina markdown / Crawl4AI) with no <script> blocks — honestly empty,
1980:    never fabricated.
1981:
1982:    BUG-FETCH-R8d (2026-04-18): the live smoke test of
1983:    clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm showed 19/20 candidates failed via the
1984:    previous naive httpx.Client. `src/tools/access_bypass.py` already
1985:    had the full cascade (including BUG-BROTLI fix, concurrent Crawl4AI
1986:    /Jina/Firecrawl, paywall detection) but pipeline A wasn't wired
1987:    to it. This is the wiring.
1988:
1989:    The AccessBypass call is async; live_retriever's fetch loop is
1990:    sync by historical choice. We run the async call in a fresh event
1991:    loop per URL. Full async refactor of run_live_retrieval (with
1992:    asyncio.gather for concurrency) is tracked as R-RETRIEVE-ASYNC in
1993:    docs/todo_list.md.
1994:
1995:    Env opt-out: set PG_DISABLE_ACCESS_BYPASS=1 to fall back to the
1996:    naive httpx path (useful when Playwright/Crawl4AI is unavailable).
1997:
1998:    I-meta-007c: ``doi_hint`` / ``pmid_hint`` are optional identifiers carried
1999:    from the candidate's S2 metadata. When AccessBypass returns stub/empty/
2000:    paywalled content AND a DOI is resolvable (hint or extracted from the URL),
2001:    the OPEN-ACCESS resolver (Unpaywall v2 -> AccessBypass; else PubMed EFetch
2002:    abstract) is tried BEFORE giving up. Gated by PG_ENABLE_LIVE_OA_RESOLVER
2003:    (default "1"); fail-OPEN (resolver errors never break retrieval).
2004:    """
2005:    # I-meta-007b: single wall-clock for the tool tracer (record-only). The
2006:    # naive-httpx fallbacks below are recorded as the content-fetch outcome so
2007:    # the per-run summary reflects every fetch attempt's path + latency.
2008:    _t0 = time.time()
2009:    if os.getenv("PG_DISABLE_ACCESS_BYPASS", "0") == "1":
2010:        # M-45 pass-2: record env-opt-out so diagnostics can see it.
2011:        _m45_record_fetch_telemetry(
2012:            url, "httpx_naive", "pg_disable_access_bypass=1"
2013:        )
2014:        # I-meta-007b P2a: record the FINAL fallback outcome (ok/fail), not the
2015:        # pre-fallback "fail" — a successful naive fetch must be recorded ok.
2016:        return _fallback_naive_fetch(
2017:            url, max_chars, _t0, "pg_disable_access_bypass=1"
2018:        )
2019:    try:
2020:        from src.tools.access_bypass import AccessBypass
2021:    except Exception as exc:
2022:        logger.warning(
2023:            "[live_retriever] AccessBypass unavailable (%s); "
2024:            "falling back to naive httpx", exc,
2025:        )
2026:        _m45_record_fetch_telemetry(
2027:            url, "httpx_naive", f"access_bypass_import_failed: {exc}"
2028:        )
2029:        # I-meta-007b P2a: record the FINAL fallback outcome (ok/fail).
2030:        return _fallback_naive_fetch(
2031:            url, max_chars, _t0, f"access_bypass_import_failed: {exc}"
2032:        )
2033:
2034:    # Run AccessBypass in a dedicated thread so each call gets its own
2035:    # fresh event loop. This works whether we're called from sync or
2036:    # async context (expansion path runs inside a live loop). Crawl4AI
2037:    # leaves background tasks that make subsequent asyncio.run() in the
2038:    # same thread fail with "cannot be called from a running event loop".
2039:    #
2040:    # BB5-S02 (#1177): import the cross-thread in-flight bound + leak gauge +
2041:    # the wedged-task-draining runner. The bound is acquired INSIDE the worker
2042:    # (so an abandoned worker holds its slot until it truly terminates) and
2043:    # released in the worker's OWN finally — never in the outer join path
2044:    # (releasing on abandonment over-releases; not releasing leaks the slot).
2045:    from src.tools.access_bypass import (
2046:        _get_bypass_inflight_semaphore,
2047:        polaris_asyncio_run,
2048:        record_bypass_leaked_worker,
2049:    )
2050:    result_holder: dict[str, Any] = {}
2051:    _inflight_sem = _get_bypass_inflight_semaphore()
2052:    # BB5-S02 iter-3 (#1177, Codex P1 continuing): a per-worker ABANDONED flag.
2053:    # The outer join-timeout path SETS it; the worker CHECKS it twice — (a)
2054:    # immediately after acquiring the in-flight slot (closes the
2055:    # blocked-on-semaphore window: a worker that timed out while STILL queued on
2056:    # `_inflight_sem.acquire()` must NOT proceed to run AccessBypass / spawn a
2057:    # browser once the slot finally frees) and (b) right after publishing its
2058:    # loop (closes the narrow TOCTOU between passing check (a) and the outer path
2059:    # reading a not-yet-published loop). Under the GIL the handshake is closed:
2060:    # worker = publish-loop-then-check-flag; outer = set-flag-then-read-loop.
2061:    _abandoned = threading.Event()
2062:
2063:    def _bypass_worker() -> None:
2064:        # BB5-S02: acquire a cross-thread in-flight slot. Bounds the number of
2065:        # concurrently-LIVE bypass workers (each may hold a browser subprocess)
2066:        # across all per-thread event loops — `_get_crawl4ai_semaphore` cannot,
2067:        # being lazy-bound to THIS thread's fresh loop only.
2068:        _inflight_sem.acquire()
2069:        try:
2070:            # BB5-S02 iter-3 (#1177, Codex P1 continuing): if this worker was
2071:            # abandoned (outer join timed out) WHILE it was blocked here on
2072:            # `acquire()`, bail BEFORE constructing AccessBypass / publishing a
2073:            # loop / spawning a browser. The outer path could not cancel us (no
2074:            # loop was ever published), so the worker itself must self-abort —
2075:            # otherwise a stale fetch starts AFTER its caller already fell back,
2076:            # consuming a slot + browser resources. `return` here lets the
2077:            # existing `finally` perform the single in-flight-slot release (do
2078:            # NOT release explicitly — a BoundedSemaphore double-release raises).
2079:            if _abandoned.is_set():
2080:                return
2081:            bypass = AccessBypass()
2082:
2083:            async def _capture_loop_and_fetch() -> Any:
2084:                # BB5-S02 iter-2 (#1177, Codex P1-S02): publish THIS worker's
2085:                # running loop into the shared holder as the FIRST awaited step,
2086:                # BEFORE the (potentially wedging) fetch. The outer join path
2087:                # reads this loop to actively signal teardown on abandonment.
2088:                # Capturing here — inside the coro rather than by changing
2089:                # polaris_asyncio_run — keeps the drain-runner's signature (and
2090:                # its spy test) untouched.
2091:                result_holder["loop"] = asyncio.get_running_loop()
2092:                # BB5-S02 iter-3 (#1177): second abandonment check, AFTER the
2093:                # loop is published. Closes the TOCTOU where the worker passed
2094:                # the post-acquire check, then the outer path timed out and read
2095:                # `loop` as None (not yet published) → no teardown was sent.
2096:                # Raising CancelledError (vs returning) matches the worker's
2097:                # existing `except asyncio.CancelledError` handler and skips the
2098:                # fetch entirely, so no stale browser op starts.
2099:                if _abandoned.is_set():
2100:                    raise asyncio.CancelledError(
2101:                        "bypass worker abandoned before fetch start"
2102:                    )
2103:                return await bypass.fetch_with_bypass(url, prefer_legal=True)
2104:
2105:            # BB5-S02: polaris_asyncio_run (vs bare asyncio.run) force-drains
2106:            # wedged detached Playwright fetch tasks BEFORE the loop's
2107:            # cancel-all phase, so the loop teardown cannot hang on an
2108:            # un-cancellable browser op — closing the orphan-subprocess window
2109:            # for a worker that DOES eventually return.
2110:            result_holder["value"] = polaris_asyncio_run(_capture_loop_and_fetch())
2111:        except asyncio.CancelledError:
2112:            # BB5-S02 iter-2: the outer join path actively cancelled this loop's
2113:            # tasks on abandonment (active teardown). CancelledError is a
2114:            # BaseException — NOT caught by `except Exception` — so catch it
2115:            # explicitly here, or it escapes the daemon thread as a noisy
2116:            # traceback. The slot still releases in the finally below.
2117:            result_holder["error"] = result_holder.get("error") or RuntimeError(
2118:                "bypass worker cancelled on abandonment teardown"
2119:            )
2120:        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
2121:            result_holder["error"] = exc
2122:        finally:
2123:            _inflight_sem.release()
2124:
2125:    worker = threading.Thread(target=_bypass_worker, daemon=True)
2126:    worker.start()
2127:    # BUG-FETCH-R8d medium-1 (Codex pass 4): bound the join so a wedged
2128:    # Crawl4AI/Playwright browser startup/cleanup can't hang the sweep
2129:    # indefinitely. AccessBypass has internal timeouts but they don't
2130:    # cover every subprocess/import/browser-cleanup failure mode.
2131:    # Default 90s = Crawl4AI worst-case (~70s) + margin. Override via
2132:    # PG_FETCH_DEADLINE_SECONDS. Set to 0 to disable (not recommended).
2133:    try:
2134:        deadline = float(os.getenv("PG_FETCH_DEADLINE_SECONDS", "90"))
2135:    except ValueError:
2136:        deadline = 90.0
2137:    worker.join(timeout=deadline if deadline > 0 else None)
2138:    if worker.is_alive():
2139:        # BB5-S02 iter-3 (#1177, Codex P1 continuing): SET the abandoned flag
2140:        # FIRST — before the gauge/log/loop-read — so a worker about to wake
2141:        # from a blocked `_inflight_sem.acquire()` (or about to publish its
2142:        # loop) observes the abandonment ASAP and self-aborts WITHOUT starting
2143:        # AccessBypass. This closes the pre-loop window the active loop-cancel
2144:        # teardown below cannot reach (that teardown needs a published loop;
2145:        # a still-queued worker has none). The two mechanisms compose: this
2146:        # flag covers the never-started (pre-loop) case; the loop cancel below
2147:        # covers the in-flight (post-loop) case.
2148:        _abandoned.set()
2149:        # BB5-S02 (#1177): the worker is ABANDONED (still alive past the join
2150:        # deadline) — it holds its in-flight slot + possibly a live browser
2151:        # subprocess until it finally terminates. Record the leak gauge so the
2152:        # accumulated-orphan-subprocess signal is auditable (was silent before).
2153:        _leaked = record_bypass_leaked_worker()
2154:        logger.warning(
2155:            "[live_retriever] AccessBypass timed out after %.0fs for %s "
2156:            "— falling back to naive httpx (thread abandoned as daemon; "
2157:            "leaked_bypass_workers=%d)",
2158:            deadline, url[:80], _leaked,
2159:        )
2160:        # BB5-S02 iter-2 (#1177, Codex P1-S02): the previous code only RECORDED
2161:        # the abandonment (gauge) — it never FORCED the wedged worker to release
2162:        # its browser. Here we ACTIVELY signal teardown: cancel every task on the
2163:        # abandoned worker's event loop. The fetch coro was created via
2164:        # `loop.create_task` inside polaris_asyncio_run, so cancelling it injects
2165:        # CancelledError at the wedged `await`, which runs _try_crawl4ai's
2166:        # `finally: await _safe_close_crawler(...)` — closing the Crawl4AI/
2167:        # Playwright browser context instead of orphaning it. `call_soon_thread-
2168:        # safe` is the ONLY thread-safe way to touch another loop; `all_tasks`
2169:        # then runs IN that loop thread where it is safe. Fire-and-forget — we do
2170:        # NOT join the worker, preserving the non-hang property of this fallback.
2171:        #
2172:        # Honest limitation (mirrors the trafilatura SIGSEGV mitigation note): a
2173:        # worker wedged in a synchronous C-level Playwright call observes the
2174:        # cancellation only when it next yields to its loop. This is inherent to
2175:        # cooperative cancellation and cannot be engineered past in-process; the
2176:        # signal is still sent so a cancellable wait tears down promptly.
2177:        _abandoned_loop = result_holder.get("loop")
2178:        if _abandoned_loop is not None:
2179:            try:
2180:                if not _abandoned_loop.is_closed():
2181:                    def _cancel_all_on_abandoned_loop(
2182:                        _loop: Any = _abandoned_loop,
2183:                    ) -> None:
2184:                        try:
2185:                            for _task in asyncio.all_tasks(_loop):
2186:                                _task.cancel()
2187:                        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — best-effort teardown
2188:                            pass
2189:
2190:                    _abandoned_loop.call_soon_threadsafe(
2191:                        _cancel_all_on_abandoned_loop
2192:                    )
2193:            except RuntimeError:
2194:                # Loop closed/finished racing with the cancel request — the
2195:                # worker already returned on its own; nothing to tear down.
2196:                pass
2197:        # M-45 pass-2: record AccessBypass timeout so diagnostics can
2198:        # distinguish timeout from backend refusal.
2199:        _m45_record_fetch_telemetry(
2200:            url, "httpx_naive", f"access_bypass_timeout_{int(deadline)}s",
2201:        )
2202:        # I-meta-007b P2a: record the FINAL fallback outcome (ok/fail). The
2203:        # backend that actually ran is httpx_naive, not access_bypass.
2204:        return _fallback_naive_fetch(
2205:            url, max_chars, _t0, f"access_bypass_timeout_{int(deadline)}s"
2206:        )
2207:
2208:    if "error" in result_holder:
2209:        exc = result_holder["error"]
2210:        logger.warning(
2211:            "[live_retriever] AccessBypass raised for %s: %s: %s",
2212:            url[:80], type(exc).__name__, exc,
2213:        )
2214:        _m45_record_fetch_telemetry(
2215:            url, "httpx_naive",
2216:            f"access_bypass_raised_{type(exc).__name__}",
2217:        )
2218:        # I-meta-007b P2a: record the FINAL fallback outcome (ok/fail).
2219:        return _fallback_naive_fetch(
2220:            url, max_chars, _t0, f"access_bypass_raised_{type(exc).__name__}"
2221:        )
2222:    if "value" not in result_holder:
2223:        logger.warning(
2224:            "[live_retriever] AccessBypass produced no result for %s",
2225:            url[:80],
2226:        )
2227:        _m45_record_fetch_telemetry(
2228:            url, "httpx_naive", "access_bypass_no_result"
2229:        )
2230:        # I-meta-007b P2a: record the FINAL fallback outcome (ok/fail).
2231:        return _fallback_naive_fetch(
2232:            url, max_chars, _t0, "access_bypass_no_result"
2233:        )
2234:    result = result_holder["value"]
2235:
2236:    method = getattr(result, "access_method", "unknown") or "unknown"
2237:    if not result.success or not result.content:
2238:        reason = (result.metadata or {}).get("reason") if hasattr(result, "metadata") else None
2239:        logger.info(
2240:            "[live_retriever] fetch_miss %s (method=%s reason=%s)",
2241:            url[:80], method, reason or "no_content",
2242:        )
2243:        # M-45 pass-2: record the winning backend + reason even on miss
2244:        # so downstream audits can see which backend was last invoked.
2245:        _m45_record_fetch_telemetry(url, method, reason or "no_content")
2246:        # I-meta-007c: AccessBypass returned a stub/empty/paywalled result.
2247:        # Before giving up, try the OPEN-ACCESS resolver when a DOI is
2248:        # available (from S2 metadata hint or embedded in the URL). On a hit
2249:        # we return the upgraded content with ok=True so the longer body
2250:        # re-tiers above T7 downstream. Fail-OPEN: an empty resolver result
2251:        # leaves the existing stub return path untouched.
2252:        #
2253:        # Diff-gate P2b: when the resolver is gated OFF, do NOT compute the DOI
2254:        # and do NOT call _try_oa_resolution — the OFF path is byte-identical to
2255:        # the pre-existing stub control flow (resolver never invoked).
2256:        # Diff-gate P1: enforce the DOI-present gate at the CALL SITE. Only enter
2257:        # the OA-resolver branch when a non-empty DOI is resolvable (hint or
2258:        # embedded in the URL). An empty-DOI candidate (e.g. an Europe-PMC record
2259:        # with doi=None + pmid set) must NOT be upgraded via a PMID-only PubMed
2260:        # fetch — it falls straight through to the pre-existing miss tuple.
2261:        if _oa_resolver_enabled():
2262:            oa_doi = (doi_hint or "").strip() or _extract_doi_from_url(url)
2263:            if oa_doi:
2264:                oa_content = _try_oa_resolution(
2265:                    url=url,
2266:                    extracted_doi=oa_doi,
2267:                    pmid=(pmid_hint or "").strip(),
2268:                    max_chars=max_chars,
2269:                )
2270:                if oa_content:
2271:                    logger.info(
2272:                        "[live_retriever] fetch_oa %s (doi=%s chars=%d) — "
2273:                        "upgraded from stub via OA resolver",
2274:                        url[:80], oa_doi, len(oa_content),
2275:                    )
2276:                    _m45_record_fetch_telemetry(url, "oa_resolver", "")
2277:                    _trace_tool(
2278:                        "fetch_content", target=url, status="ok",
2279:                        latency_ms=(time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0,
2280:                        backend_used="oa_resolver",
2281:                        bytes_received=len(oa_content),
2282:                        content_length=len(oa_content),
2283:                    )
2284:                    return oa_content, True, "", "", ""
2285:        _trace_tool(
2286:            "fetch_content", target=url, status="stub",
2287:            latency_ms=(time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0,
2288:            backend_used=method, error=str(reason or "no_content"),
2289:        )
2290:        return "", False, "", "", ""
2291:    # BUG-M-14 (Codex pass 14): extract the full page title from the
2292:    # raw result.content BEFORE _strip_html removes <title> tags. Jina
2293:    # markdown has "Title: X" on first line; Crawl4AI cleaned text has
2294:    # the same. HTML fetches have <title>.
2295:    extracted_title = _extract_title_from_content(result.content)
2296:    # BUG-M-17 (Codex pass 2): detect article-type from body.
2297:    body_type = _detect_article_type_from_body(result.content)
2298:    # Diff-gate P1-C: capture raw ld+json from result.content BEFORE _strip_html
2299:    # removes <script> blocks. Direct-HTTP path returns raw HTML (JSON-LD present);
2300:    # Jina/Crawl4AI return cleaned text with <script> already gone (honestly empty).
2301:    jsonld = _extract_jsonld_blocks(result.content)
2302:    # result.content is already extracted (Jina = markdown, Crawl4AI =
2303:    # cleaned text). _strip_html is a safety net for direct-HTTP path
2304:    # which returns raw HTML.
2305:    content = _strip_html(result.content)[:max_chars]
2306:    # BB5-C05 (#1177): "fetched-200-but-empty-extract" — the backend fetched
2307:    # real content (success + non-empty result.content) yet the extractor chain
2308:    # (trafilatura → readability → regex) collapsed it below the usable floor.
2309:    # This is a DISTINCT failure class from a network miss / paywall stub, and
2310:    # was previously swallowed silently (counted as a generic miss). Surface it
2311:    # as its own telemetry bucket so it is auditable, not silent.
2312:    _raw_len = len(result.content or "")
2313:    if _raw_len >= _EXTRACT_NONEMPTY_RAW_FLOOR and len(content) < _EXTRACT_EMPTY_FLOOR:
2314:        logger.info(
2315:            "[live_retriever] fetched_200_but_empty_extract %s "
2316:            "(method=%s raw_chars=%d extracted_chars=%d)",
2317:            url[:80], method, _raw_len, len(content),
2318:        )
2319:        _m45_record_fetch_telemetry(
2320:            url, method, "fetched_200_but_empty_extract",
2321:        )
2322:        _trace_tool(
2323:            "fetch_content", target=url, status="empty_extract",
2324:            latency_ms=(time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0,
2325:            backend_used=method, bytes_received=len(content),
2326:            content_length=len(content),
2327:            error="fetched_200_but_empty_extract",
2328:        )
2329:        return content, bool(content), extracted_title, body_type, jsonld
2330:    logger.info(
2331:        "[live_retriever] fetch_ok %s (method=%s chars=%d)",
2332:        url[:80], method, len(content),
2333:    )
2334:    # M-45 pass-2: record winning backend for diagnostics.
2335:    _m45_record_fetch_telemetry(url, method, "")
2336:    _trace_tool(
2337:        "fetch_content", target=url, status="ok",
2338:        latency_ms=(time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0,
2339:        backend_used=method, bytes_received=len(content),
2340:        content_length=len(content),
2341:    )
2342:    return content, bool(content), extracted_title, body_type, jsonld

542:            payload, content_type, status = result
543:            return FetchResultRecord(
544:                source_url=task.source_url,
545:                backend_id=task.backend_id,
546:                outcome=FetchOutcome.SUCCESS,
547:                payload=bytes(payload),
548:                content_type=content_type,
549:                fetch_status_code=status,
550:                error=None,
551:                started_at=task_started,
552:                finished_at=time.time(),
553:                task_metadata=task.task_metadata,
554:            )
555:        finally:
556:            sem.release()
557:
558:    results_by_index: dict[int, FetchResultRecord] = {}
559:
560:    # Codex round-1 HIGH fix (v2): manage executor manually so we
561:    # can shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True) on TIMEOUT.
562:    # The `with` block's __exit__ calls shutdown(wait=True) which
563:    # blocks until all in-flight workers finish — defeating
564:    # boundary 3's caller-latency promise.
565:    executor = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(
566:        max_workers=max_workers,
567:    )
568:    try:
569:        future_to_index: dict[concurrent.futures.Future, int] = {}
570:        # I-fetch-003 (BB5-C02): submit in ROUND-ROBIN backend order so the
571:        # first `max_workers` submissions span up to `max_workers` distinct
572:        # backends/hosts. Without this, a clustered same-host candidate prefix
573:        # makes the first workers all block on ONE host-semaphore (acquired
574:        # inside `_run_task`), starving distinct-host tasks queued behind them.
575:        # `_round_robin_indices` returns ORIGINAL deduped indices, so result
576:        # indexing into `results_by_index` is unchanged (correctness preserved).
577:        submit_order = _round_robin_indices(deduped)
578:        # I-fetch-003 (BB5-C01 P1): anchor the GLOBAL batch budget at submit.
579:        batch_start = time.monotonic()
580:        for idx in submit_order:
581:            task = deduped[idx]
582:            fut = executor.submit(_run_task, idx, task)
583:            future_to_index[fut] = idx
584:
585:        # I-fetch-003 (BB5-C01 P1): derive the GLOBAL batch budget — the
586:        # termination backstop that guarantees this call returns in bounded
587:        # time even when every worker is wedged on an abandoned in-flight fetch
588:        # and the queued siblings keep their +inf per-task deadline. Env wins;
589:        # else derive from per_task_timeout and the wave count; else (no
590:        # per-task timeout AND no env) the batch budget is disabled (None),
591:        # preserving the existing "None disables timeout" contract.
592:        batch_budget = _derive_batch_budget_seconds(
593:            num_tasks=len(deduped),
594:            max_workers=max_workers,
595:            per_task_timeout=per_task_timeout,
596:        )
597:
598:        remaining: set[concurrent.futures.Future] = set(future_to_index)
599:        protocol_error_to_raise: FetcherProtocolError | None = None
600:
601:        def _effective_deadline_monotonic(idx: int) -> float:
602:            # I-fetch-003 (BB5-C01): START-anchored, MONOTONIC effective
603:            # deadline. A not-yet-started index (absent from the start map)
604:            # → +inf: it CANNOT TIMEOUT before it runs. A started index →
605:            # its OWN observed start + per_task_timeout. Caller holds
606:            # `task_started_lock`.
607:            started = task_started_monotonic_by_index.get(idx)
608:            if started is None:
609:                return float("inf")
610:            return started + per_task_timeout  # type: ignore[operator]
611:
612:        batch_deadline = (
613:            batch_start + batch_budget if batch_budget is not None else None
614:        )
615:
616:        while remaining and protocol_error_to_raise is None:
617:            if per_task_timeout is None and batch_deadline is None:
618:                # No per-task timeout AND no batch budget — just wait for the
619:                # next completion (the original "None disables timeout" path).
620:                done, _ = concurrent.futures.wait(
621:                    remaining,
622:                    return_when=concurrent.futures.FIRST_COMPLETED,
623:                )
624:            else:
625:                # I-fetch-003 (BB5-C01): BOUNDED-POLL harvest. The wait is
626:                # ALWAYS bounded by `_HARVEST_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS`, never
627:                # `timeout=None`, so a task that STARTS after this snapshot
628:                # (or one that hangs), AND the GLOBAL batch deadline, are
629:                # re-checked within the poll interval — the harvest never
630:                # relies on a sibling completing to make progress. When there
631:                # is no per-task timeout (batch budget only), the per-task
632:                # deadlines are all +inf so the poll interval alone bounds the
633:                # wait until the batch deadline fires.
634:                now_mono = time.monotonic()
635:                if per_task_timeout is None:
636:                    next_deadline = float("inf")
637:                else:
638:                    with task_started_lock:
639:                        next_deadline = min(
640:                            _effective_deadline_monotonic(future_to_index[f])
641:                            for f in remaining
642:                        )
643:                if batch_deadline is not None:
644:                    next_deadline = min(next_deadline, batch_deadline)
645:                wait_timeout = min(
646:                    max(0.0, next_deadline - now_mono),
647:                    _HARVEST_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS,
648:                )
649:                done, _ = concurrent.futures.wait(
650:                    remaining,
651:                    timeout=wait_timeout,
652:                    return_when=concurrent.futures.FIRST_COMPLETED,
653:                )
654:
655:            if done:
656:                for fut in done:
657:                    idx = future_to_index[fut]
658:                    task = deduped[idx]
659:                    try:
660:                        rec = fut.result()
661:                    except FetcherProtocolError as exc:
662:                        protocol_error_to_raise = exc
663:                        break
664:                    results_by_index[idx] = rec
665:                    remaining.discard(fut)
666:
667:            if protocol_error_to_raise is not None:
668:                break
669:
670:            if per_task_timeout is not None:
671:                # Recompute the expired set from CURRENT start-times after
672:                # every wake (whether or not anything completed). A task is
673:                # TIMEOUT iff IT exceeded ITS OWN start-anchored budget.
674:                now_mono = time.monotonic()
675:                with task_started_lock:
676:                    expired = [
677:                        f for f in list(remaining)
678:                        if _effective_deadline_monotonic(
679:                            future_to_index[f]
680:                        ) <= now_mono
681:                    ]
682:                if expired:
683:                    now_wall = time.time()
684:                    for fut in expired:
685:                        idx = future_to_index[fut]
686:                        task = deduped[idx]
687:                        fut.cancel()  # best-effort
688:                        with task_started_lock:
689:                            timeout_started_at = (
690:                                task_started_wall_by_index.get(idx, now_wall)

exec

699:                            error="per-task timeout exceeded",

701:                            finished_at=now_wall,
702:                            task_metadata=task.task_metadata,
703:                        )
704:                        remaining.discard(fut)
705:
706:            # I-fetch-003 (BB5-C01 P1): GLOBAL batch-budget termination
707:            # guarantee. If the batch deadline has passed, the batch did NOT
708:            # converge in bounded time — almost always because every worker is
709:            # wedged on an abandoned (timed-out but un-cancellable) in-flight
710:            # fetch while distinct-host siblings sit queued behind it forever.
711:            # Record EVERY still-remaining future (not just never-started ones,
712:            # or the final `results_by_index[i]` reduction KeyErrors on a
713:            # started-but-unfinished index) and break so the call ALWAYS
714:            # returns:
715:            #   - started-but-unfinished (idx in the start map) → TIMEOUT (it
716:            #     ran and over-ran; same class as a normal per-task timeout).
717:            #   - never-started (idx absent from the start map) → NOT_DISPATCHED
718:            #     (it never reached the fetcher; distinct outcome, NOT a
719:            #     mislabelled TIMEOUT).
720:            if (
721:                batch_deadline is not None
722:                and remaining
723:                and time.monotonic() > batch_deadline
724:            ):
725:                now_wall = time.time()
726:                for fut in list(remaining):
727:                    idx = future_to_index[fut]
728:                    task = deduped[idx]
729:                    fut.cancel()  # best-effort
730:                    with task_started_lock:
731:                        was_started = (
732:                            idx in task_started_monotonic_by_index
733:                        )
734:                        started_wall = task_started_wall_by_index.get(
735:                            idx, now_wall,
736:                        )
737:                    if was_started:
738:                        results_by_index[idx] = FetchResultRecord(
739:                            source_url=task.source_url,
740:                            backend_id=task.backend_id,
741:                            outcome=FetchOutcome.TIMEOUT,
742:                            payload=None,
743:                            content_type=None,
744:                            fetch_status_code=None,
745:                            error="per-task timeout exceeded",
746:                            started_at=started_wall,
747:                            finished_at=now_wall,
748:                            task_metadata=task.task_metadata,
749:                        )
750:                    else:
751:                        results_by_index[idx] = FetchResultRecord(
752:                            source_url=task.source_url,
753:                            backend_id=task.backend_id,
754:                            outcome=FetchOutcome.NOT_DISPATCHED,
755:                            payload=None,
756:                            content_type=None,
757:                            fetch_status_code=None,
758:                            error=(
759:                                "never dispatched: parallel-fetch batch "
760:                                "budget exceeded (worker pool starved by "
761:                                "abandoned in-flight tasks)"
762:                            ),
763:                            started_at=now_wall,
764:                            finished_at=now_wall,
765:                            task_metadata=task.task_metadata,
766:                        )
767:                    remaining.discard(fut)
768:                break
769:
770:        if protocol_error_to_raise is not None:
771:            for other_fut in remaining:
772:                other_fut.cancel()
773:            # Codex round-1 MEDIUM fix (v2): release the executor
774:            # without waiting so the protocol error propagates
775:            # promptly. In-flight workers continue but caller
776:            # gets control back.
777:            executor.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
778:            raise protocol_error_to_raise
779:    finally:
780:        # Codex round-1 HIGH fix (v2): non-blocking shutdown.
781:        # Pending (not-yet-started) futures are cancelled;
782:        # in-flight workers continue but don't gate our return.
783:        executor.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
784:
785:    # Reorder by input index.
786:    results = tuple(
787:        results_by_index[i] for i in range(len(deduped))
788:    )
789:
790:    success = sum(1 for r in results if r.outcome == FetchOutcome.SUCCESS)

181:
182:def _family_from_slug(slug: str) -> str:
183:    """The family lineage of an OpenRouter slug = its `provider/` prefix (fail loud if absent).
184:
185:    Every OpenRouter slug is `provider/model`; the provider prefix IS the family lineage the
186:    4-distinct-family invariant is asserted over (z-ai / ibm-granite / qwen / deepseek). A slug
187:    with no `/` cannot have its family determined — raise rather than guess (LAW II fail-loud).
188:    """
189:    provider, sep, _ = slug.partition("/")
190:    if not sep or not provider:
191:        raise ValueError(
192:            f"benchmark verifier slug {slug!r} is not a `provider/model` OpenRouter slug; its "
193:            "family lineage cannot be determined (fail-closed — the 4-distinct-family invariant "
194:            "must assert on a real family)."
195:        )
196:    return provider
197:
198:
199:def benchmark_verifier_slug(role: str) -> str:
200:    """Resolve a verifier role's BENCHMARK-STAGE OpenRouter slug (P1-1, LAW VI).
201:
202:    Sources `PG_<ROLE>_MODEL` (the lock's per-role `env_vars.primary`), DEFAULTING to the
203:    operator-chosen role_selection_final.md OpenRouter slug. This is the benchmark lineup —
204:    DISTINCT from the lock's self-host `_lock_model_slug` for Mirror + Sentinel (see module
205:    docstring). The generator/unknown role raises (it is not served by this transport).
206:    """
207:    if role == _EXCLUDED_ROLE:
208:        raise ValueError(
209:            f"role {role!r} is not served by this transport — the generator runs on OpenRouter "
210:            "via openrouter_client.py, upstream of the per-claim verifier path."
211:        )
212:    if role not in _SERVED_ROLES:
213:        raise ValueError(
214:            f"role {role!r} is not a benchmark-stage verifier role {_SERVED_ROLES}"
215:        )
216:    return os.getenv(_BENCHMARK_LINEUP_ENV[role], _BENCHMARK_LINEUP_DEFAULT_SLUG[role])
217:
218:
219:def benchmark_verifier_lineup() -> dict[str, str]:
220:    """Return the active `{mirror,sentinel,judge: benchmark_slug}` map (P1-1, LAW VI).
221:
222:    The SINGLE source of truth for the benchmark-stage verifier slugs, consumed by BOTH the
223:    transport's `openrouter_role_endpoint` (the slug it POSTs as `body["model"]`) AND
224:    run_gate_b.py's `verifier_model_slugs` (the slug it pins into `RoleRequest.model_slug`), so
225:    served (the slug sent) == pinned (the slug in the request/gate) on the OpenRouter path.
226:    """
227:    return {role: benchmark_verifier_slug(role) for role in _SERVED_ROLES}
228:
229:
230:def benchmark_verifier_family(role: str) -> str:
231:    """Return the ACTIVE BENCHMARK-lineup family for a verifier `role` (the active-lineup family
232:    the 4-distinct-family check asserts on, NOT the lock's). The generator/unknown role raises.
233:
234:    The family is derived from the ACTIVE slug (`benchmark_verifier_slug`, which honors the
235:    `PG_<ROLE>_MODEL` override) — NOT a static assumption. FAILS LOUD when an override's family
236:    leaves the role's expected default lane, so a cross-family re-pick (e.g.
237:    `PG_JUDGE_MODEL=z-ai/glm-5.1`, which would collide the Judge with the z-ai Mirror) is caught
238:    here rather than silently passing the 4-distinct-family gate on a stale static map
239:    (clinical-lethal self-verify, CLAUDE.md §9.1). An IN-LANE override (same provider prefix)
240:    passes.
241:    """
242:    if role == _EXCLUDED_ROLE:
243:        raise ValueError(
244:            f"role {role!r} is the generator — its family is sourced from the lock, not the "
245:            "benchmark verifier lineup."
246:        )
247:    if role not in _SERVED_ROLES:
248:        raise ValueError(
249:            f"role {role!r} is not a benchmark-stage verifier role {_SERVED_ROLES}"
250:        )
251:    active_family = _family_from_slug(benchmark_verifier_slug(role))

287:# I-arch-003 (#1253) hardening (Codex gate P2): per-role CHAIN-MIN CEILING on the resolved total
288:# max_tokens (and the Mirror numeric reasoning cap). Under provider allow_fallbacks:false the binding
289:# output cap is the MIN max_completion_tokens across the role's pinned provider chain (live OpenRouter
290:# /endpoints read 2026-06-14). A budget that EXCEEDS this — whether the default or a future bad env
291:# override — would hard-400 ("requested N > max M") on the chain; that is exactly the failure class this
292:# audit closes. So every resolved budget is clamped DOWN to its chain min (env can lower for cost/testing
293:# but never raise past the provider cap). RE-DERIVE these if a role's chain in
294:# config/settings/openrouter_provider_routing.yaml is re-pinned to higher-cap-only providers.
295:_MIRROR_MAX_TOKENS_CHAIN_MIN = 131072    # glm-5.1: atlas-cloud 202752, z-ai/baidu/novita/gmicloud >= 131072
296:_SENTINEL_MAX_TOKENS_CHAIN_MIN = 131072  # minimax-m2: google/atlas-cloud 196608, novita/minimax 131072
297:_JUDGE_MAX_TOKENS_CHAIN_MIN = 262140     # qwen3.6: wandb 262144, io-net 262140

299:# I-meta-008 / #1026: blank-verdict step-down ladder. When a reasoning-first verifier returns a
300:# BLANK bare verdict (reasoning budget exhausted without converging under the high effort), retry
301:# with the effort stepped DOWN this ladder so the model is forced to spend tokens on the VERDICT,
302:# not endless reasoning. The first entry is the configured MAX effort; `None` means reasoning
303:# DISABLED (final resort — guarantees content). Overridable via PG_FOUR_ROLE_EFFORT_LADDER
304:# (comma-separated; the literal "off"/"none" -> reasoning disabled). MAX-reasoning-first, then a
305:# guaranteed verdict, then fail-loud only if even reasoning-off blanks.
306:def _parse_effort_ladder() -> tuple[object, ...]:
307:    raw = os.getenv("PG_FOUR_ROLE_EFFORT_LADDER")
308:    if not raw:
309:        return (_REASONING_EFFORT, "low", None)
310:    out: list[object] = []
311:    for token in raw.split(","):
312:        t = token.strip()
313:        if not t:
314:            continue
315:        out.append(None if t.lower() in ("off", "none", "disabled") else t)
316:    return tuple(out) if out else (_REASONING_EFFORT, "low", None)

319:_VERIFIER_EFFORT_LADDER = _parse_effort_ladder()

321:# Per-role reasoning policy (Codex iter-2 P1 — reasoning is NOT a one-size-fits-all switch).
322:#
323:# WHY per-role: Mirror (`z-ai/glm-5.1`) and Judge (`qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b`) are DELIBERATIVE
324:# verifiers — they weigh evidence and argue a verdict, so they get MAX reasoning effort
325:# (`xhigh`) AND `provider.require_parameters=True` (only route to a provider that honors
326:# reasoning).
327:#
328:# Sentinel is MODE-AWARE (I-run11-004): the CERTIFIED MiniMax-M2 DECOMPOSITION Sentinel
329:# (`minimax/minimax-m2`, the benchmark + lock default) was certified WITH reasoning ON and
330:# max_tokens>=3000 — turning reasoning OFF (or starving max_tokens to the 256 classifier budget)
331:# truncates the JSON {verdict, atoms} mid-emission and collapses every claim to a fail-closed
332:# UNGROUNDED (the run-12 truncator). So when the active Sentinel groundedness mode is
333:# "decomposition", the Sentinel sends MAX reasoning + a GENEROUS max_tokens, exactly replicating
334:# the certified call. The SOVEREIGN `guardian`/`noninverted` granite Sentinel modes stay
335:# reasoning-OFF (a <score>/one-word classifier whose self-host slug does not advertise
336:# `reasoning`); their settings are NOT regressed. LAW VI: overridable via `PG_SENTINEL_REASONING`.
337:# I-run11-008 (#1053): Mirror keeps reasoning ON (bake-off: reasoning-OFF DOUBLED false_bind 1->2/5,
338:# i.e. a worse grounding judge), but its reasoning is BOUNDED numerically below (effort=xhigh is a
339:# no-op on GLM, so unbounded thinking exhausted max_tokens → the 47 blanks). Overridable via PG_MIRROR_REASONING.
340:_ROLE_REASONING_DEFAULT = {"mirror": True, "sentinel": False, "judge": True}

343:def sentinel_decomposition_active(slug_override: str | None = None) -> bool:
344:    """True iff the ACTIVE Sentinel groundedness mode is "decomposition" (I-run11-004).
345:
346:    Lazily resolves `sentinel_adapter.sentinel_groundedness_mode()` (env- + model-aware, LAW VI)
347:    so the transport can mode-gate the Sentinel's reasoning + max_tokens WITHOUT an import-time
348:    dependency on the adapter (the adapter does not import this transport — no cycle). Any
349:    resolution error is swallowed to the conservative non-decomposition answer (the certified
350:    MiniMax call is only requested when the mode UNAMBIGUOUSLY resolves to decomposition).
351:
352:    `slug_override` (Codex diff-gate iter-4 P2) is the slug ACTUALLY being served on this call —
353:    threaded through from the request so the reasoning/max_tokens gate keys on the real served
354:    model, not a stale `PG_SENTINEL_MODEL` env, in any direct/future call path.
355:    """
356:    try:
357:        from src.polaris_graph.roles.sentinel_adapter import (
358:            _MODE_DECOMPOSITION,
359:            sentinel_groundedness_mode,
360:        )
361:    except (ImportError, AttributeError):
362:        # I-run11-010 (#1056, D4): only a genuine lazy-IMPORT failure (module missing / symbol
363:        # renamed) is safe to swallow as "not decomposition". A ValueError from
364:        # sentinel_groundedness_mode (an UNRECOGNIZED PG_SENTINEL_GROUNDEDNESS_MODE) is a caller
365:        # config bug and must NOT be silently downgraded to the 256-token classifier path (that
366:        # truncates the certified MiniMax-M2 JSON -> the run-12 all-UNGROUNDED collapse). It now
367:        # propagates (LAW II fail-loud) instead of being swallowed by a broad `except Exception`.
368:        return False
369:    return sentinel_groundedness_mode(slug_override) == _MODE_DECOMPOSITION

372:def role_reasoning_enabled(role: str, slug_override: str | None = None) -> bool:
373:    """Whether the given verifier role should send the MAX-reasoning request params.
374:
375:    Returns the per-role default from `_ROLE_REASONING_DEFAULT`, overridable per role via the
376:    env var `PG_<ROLE>_REASONING` (LAW VI): "1"/"true"/"yes" -> True, "0"/"false"/"no" -> False;
377:    absent (or any other value) falls back to the role default. Mirror/Judge default True
378:    (deliberative verifiers -> MAX reasoning). Sentinel is MODE-AWARE (I-run11-004): in
379:    "decomposition" mode (the certified MiniMax-M2 detector) it defaults True (reasoning ON, as
380:    certified); in the sovereign granite `guardian`/`noninverted` modes it defaults False (a
381:    classifier whose slug does not advertise `reasoning`, so reasoning would break routing).
382:    """
383:    if role == "sentinel" and os.getenv(f"PG_{role.upper()}_REASONING") is None:
384:        # No explicit override: the Sentinel default is mode-aware (decomposition -> reasoning ON),
385:        # keyed on the ACTUAL served slug when supplied (Codex diff-gate iter-4 P2).
386:        default = sentinel_decomposition_active(slug_override)
387:    else:
388:        default = _ROLE_REASONING_DEFAULT.get(role, False)
389:    override = os.getenv(f"PG_{role.upper()}_REASONING")
390:    if override is None:
391:        return default
392:    token = override.strip().lower()
393:    if token in ("1", "true", "yes"):
394:        return True
395:    if token in ("0", "false", "no"):
396:        return False
397:    return default

400:# Reuse the same timeout knob the self-host transport + openrouter_client use (LAW VI).
401:# I-meta-008 FULL-POWER: the reasoning verifiers (Mirror/Judge at effort=xhigh against a 16384-token
402:# budget) take MINUTES per claim — give them their OWN generous timeout (default 900s/15min), not the
403:# cheap 90s shared default (which also governs retrieval/embeddings). Env-overridable (LAW VI).
404:# NOTE: this is the PER-POST httpx timeout (bounds ONE network call). It does NOT bound the COMPOSED
405:# retry loop (effort-ladder x transport-retries x rate-limit-backoff), which can stack to hours per
406:# claim — that composition is bounded SEPARATELY by the #1226 wall-clock watchdog below.
407:_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = int(os.getenv("PG_VERIFIER_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "900"))
408:
409:# === #1226 (I-pipe-001): blank-content bound + per-call wall-clock watchdog =====================
410:# THE FAILURE: a reasoning verifier (e.g. GLM-5.1 Mirror under xhigh) can burn its whole token
411:# budget on reasoning and emit EMPTY content every attempt; combined with the per-POST 900s timeout
412:# (_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) the COMPOSED retry loop (effort-ladder/provider-failover x transport-retries x
413:# rate-limit-backoff) can stall for HOURS per claim, so the D8 4-role gate never finishes and the
414:# whole sweep hangs. Two RELIABILITY backstops (NEVER touch a verify/NLI/4-role verdict; on
415:# exhaustion they re-raise the EXISTING fail-loud BlankVerdictError/RoleTransportError -> release
416:# stays HELD; no fake/empty verdict is ever synthesized):
417:#
418:#   (1) PG_ROLE_BLANK_MAX_RETRIES (default 3): a HARD ceiling on the number of blank-content RETRIES.
419:#       Implemented by TRUNCATING the per-call effort/provider ladder to `1 + max_retries` attempts.
420:#       At the default (1 + 3 = 4) this ceiling is >= every role's existing default ladder length
421:#       (Judge 3, Mirror/routed-Sentinel 1+PG_PROVIDER_BLANK_RETRIES=4, classifier-Sentinel 1,
422:#       decomposition-Sentinel 3), so the truncation is the IDENTITY at default — it only bites when
423:#       a custom PG_PROVIDER_BLANK_RETRIES / PG_FOUR_ROLE_EFFORT_LADDER pushes a ladder past the cap.
424:#       Exhaustion fires the SAME existing `raise` at ladder-end -> the existing BlankVerdictError.
425:#
426:#   (2) PG_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S (default 3600s/1h): a COOPERATIVE wall-clock watchdog over the whole
427:#       `complete()` retry loop (checked at the top of each attempt + before each rate-limit sleep).
428:#       It does NOT interrupt an in-flight POST (httpx's _TIMEOUT_SECONDS bounds that) — it bounds the
429:#       COMPOSITION so a stuck-in-retries call aborts LOUDLY instead of hanging the sweep. 1h is a
430:#       deliberately-generous BACKSTOP against a wedged call, NOT a tight SLO: it sits well above any
431:#       HEALTHY 4-role call (a healthy claim resolves in seconds-to-minutes — the ladder rarely runs
432:#       to exhaustion, and a single blanking call returns its empty 200 quickly, it does not consume
433:#       the full per-POST 900s). The worst-case LEGITIMATE composition (every rung waiting near the
434:#       full _TIMEOUT_SECONDS, plus transport-retries and rate-limit backoff) CAN approach/exceed 1h;
435:#       that path is already pathological (it IS the stall this fix targets), so aborting it loudly is
436:#       the intended behavior, and the operator RAISES PG_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S if a slower legitimate
437:#       budget is ever needed (LAW VI). On trip it re-raises the last BlankVerdictError (if the loop
438:#       was blanking) else a RoleTransportError (both fail-loud; never a synthesized verdict).
439:#
440:# KILL-SWITCH (default-ON correct fix): PG_ROLE_BLANK_WATCHDOG (default "1"). Set "0"/"false"/"no" to

 succeeded in 1095ms:
455:    control flow); absent or anything else -> ON (the default-ON correct fix). LAW VI: env-driven,
456:    read lazily so a runtime override is honored.
457:    """
458:    token = os.getenv(_ROLE_BLANK_WATCHDOG_ENV, "1").strip().lower()
459:    return token not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")
460:
461:
462:def role_blank_max_retries() -> int:
463:    """The #1226 hard ceiling on blank-content RETRIES (default 3 -> 1+3=4 total attempts).
464:
465:    Clamped to >= 0 (a negative env can never produce a sub-1 attempt budget). The ladder is
466:    truncated to `1 + role_blank_max_retries()` attempts. LAW VI: env-driven, read lazily.
467:    """
468:    return max(0, int(os.getenv(_ROLE_BLANK_MAX_RETRIES_ENV, _ROLE_BLANK_MAX_RETRIES_DEFAULT)))
469:
470:
471:def role_call_timeout_seconds() -> float:
472:    """The #1226 wall-clock watchdog budget for the WHOLE `complete()` retry loop (default 3600s).
473:
474:    Clamped to > 0 (a non-positive value would abort every call immediately); a non-positive or
475:    unparseable override falls back to the generous default. LAW VI: env-driven, read lazily.
476:    """
477:    raw = os.getenv(_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S_ENV, _ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S_DEFAULT)
478:    try:
479:        value = float(raw)
480:    except (TypeError, ValueError):
481:        return float(_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S_DEFAULT)
482:    return value if value > 0 else float(_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S_DEFAULT)

484:
485:def _watchdog_expired(start_monotonic: float, timeout_s: float, now_monotonic: float) -> bool:
486:    """Pure predicate: has `(now - start)` exceeded the watchdog budget? (testable in isolation).
487:
488:    Uses a monotonic clock delta (never wall-clock, so an NTP step cannot spuriously trip it).
489:    Returns True iff the elapsed time strictly exceeds `timeout_s`.
490:    """
491:    return (now_monotonic - start_monotonic) > timeout_s
492:
493:# I-beatboth-429 (#1173): a per-claim judge burst (~178 calls for drb_72) trips OpenRouter's
494:# rate limit on the qwen judge — a 429 (or a transient 503) on a SINGLE role call must NOT hold
495:# the 4-role release. Bounded exponential backoff-retry on the retryable HTTP statuses, honoring
496:# the `Retry-After` response header, is RESILIENCE ONLY: exhausted retries STILL raise
497:# RoleTransportError below -> release HELD (fail-closed; the gate is never weakened). LAW VI:
498:# every knob is env-driven (named, no magic numbers), read LAZILY per-call (mirrors the adjacent
499:# PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_RETRIES pattern so an override set after import is honored).
500:_ROLE_HTTP_RETRY_MAX_DEFAULT = "5"
501:_ROLE_HTTP_RETRY_STATUS_DEFAULT = "429,503"
502:_ROLE_HTTP_BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS_DEFAULT = "2.0"
503:_ROLE_HTTP_BACKOFF_CAP_SECONDS_DEFAULT = "60.0"
504:
505:
506:def _parse_retry_after_seconds(value: str | None) -> float | None:
507:    """Parse the `Retry-After` header as integer seconds (RFC 9110 delta-seconds form).
508:
509:    Returns the non-negative float seconds on success, or `None` when the header is absent,
510:    empty, or a non-numeric/HTTP-date form — in which case the caller falls back to the capped
511:    exponential backoff. We deliberately do NOT parse the HTTP-date form (per the I-beatboth-429
512:    spec): any non-integer value is treated as the absent case so a malformed header can never
513:    yield a negative or unbounded sleep.
514:    """
515:    if value is None:
516:        return None
517:    try:
518:        seconds = int(value.strip())
519:    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
520:        return None
521:    return float(seconds) if seconds >= 0 else None

523:# LAW VI: base URL + key come from the SAME env vars openrouter_client reads (single source of
524:# truth). Read lazily (function-level) so import never depends on env presence.
525:_BASE_URL_ENV = "OPENROUTER_BASE_URL"
526:_BASE_URL_DEFAULT = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
527:_API_KEY_ENV = "OPENROUTER_API_KEY"

530:def openrouter_base_url() -> str:
531:    """The OpenRouter API base URL (LAW VI: OPENROUTER_BASE_URL env, openrouter_client default).
532:
533:    Trailing slash trimmed so `{base}{_CHAT_COMPLETIONS_PATH}` is well-formed. The default
534:    already ends in `/api/v1`.
535:    """
536:    return os.getenv(_BASE_URL_ENV, _BASE_URL_DEFAULT).rstrip("/")

538:
539:def openrouter_api_key() -> str:
540:    """The OpenRouter API key (LAW VI: OPENROUTER_API_KEY env).

 succeeded in 1135ms:
620:    pass2_input / citations / system never reach the body). Then (P1-4) DROPS the top-level
621:    `documents` key, which OpenRouter's chat-completions schema does not define and which would
622:    fail routing under `provider.require_parameters=True` — the evidence is already rendered into
623:    `messages` by `_normalize_messages`, so model-visibility is preserved. Then sets
624:    `reasoning = {"enabled": True, "effort": <_REASONING_EFFORT>}` — the openrouter_client
625:    `reasoning_enabled` shape (openrouter_client.py:1424). Per OpenRouter docs the mutually
626:    exclusive pair is `reasoning.effort` vs `reasoning.max_tokens`; the TOP-LEVEL `max_tokens` is
627:    NOT exclusive with effort and MUST exceed the reasoning budget. Under `effort=xhigh` the
628:    provider spends ~95% of top-level `max_tokens` on reasoning, so a popped/absent `max_tokens`
629:    starves the verdict. I-meta-008 FULL-POWER therefore SETS a generous top-level `max_tokens`
630:    (PG_VERIFIER_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS, default 16384) for the reasoning verifiers and an explicit
631:    small classifier budget (PG_SENTINEL_MAX_TOKENS, default 256) for the non-reasoning Sentinel.
632:    Source: https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/best-practices/reasoning-tokens
633:    """
634:    body = _build_body(request, model_slug, normalized_messages)
635:    # P1-4: strip OpenRouter-unsupported top-level keys (currently `documents`). The evidence is
636:    # already model-visible in `messages` (rendered by `_normalize_messages`), so the model still
637:    # sees it; this only removes the schema-invalid body key that breaks require_parameters routing.
638:    for key in _OPENROUTER_UNSUPPORTED_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS:
639:        body.pop(key, None)
640:    # Codex iter-2 P1: reasoning is PER-ROLE. Only the deliberative verifiers (Mirror/Judge) get
641:    # MAX reasoning + `provider.require_parameters=True`. The Sentinel classifier gets NEITHER —
642:    # its OpenRouter slug does not advertise `reasoning`, so `require_parameters=True` would refuse
643:    # to route and fail the first call. See `role_reasoning_enabled` for the full rationale.
644:    if role_reasoning_enabled(request.role, request.model_slug):
645:        body["reasoning"] = {"enabled": True, "effort": _REASONING_EFFORT}
646:        # require_parameters=True makes OpenRouter ONLY route to a provider that actually HONORS the
647:        # `reasoning` param (OpenRouter's default when absent is False — it could otherwise route a
648:        # verifier to a provider that silently IGNORES reasoning, defeating the operator's
649:        # MAX-reasoning purpose). Mirrors openrouter_client._call_impl's provider block
650:        # (openrouter_client.py:1484-1487, OPENROUTER_REQUIRE_PARAMETERS default "true"). The
651:        # I-bug-946 singleton-routing half (resolved per-role provider) is M4/PENDING and
652:        # intentionally NOT here.
653:        # I-run11-007 (#1051): pin to the ranked HEALTHY provider chain (order + ignore +
654:        # allow_fallbacks:False) so a verifier call never lands on a slow/flaky provider.
655:        body["provider"] = apply_provider_routing({"require_parameters": True}, request.role)
656:        # I-meta-008 FULL-POWER (CORRECTS #1017): a reasoning verifier MUST carry a GENEROUS top-level
657:        # `max_tokens`, NOT have it popped. OpenRouter `effort=xhigh` allocates ~95% of `max_tokens`
658:        # to the reasoning budget, and `max_tokens` MUST be strictly higher than that budget so the
659:        # bare verdict has room (docs: best-practices/reasoning-tokens). It is `reasoning.effort` vs
660:        # `reasoning.max_tokens` that are mutually exclusive — NOT top-level `max_tokens`. The prior
661:        # #1017 fix popped it, so xhigh ate ~95% of an unknown provider default and STARVED the verdict
662:        # to empty for a reasoning-first Mirror (GLM-5.1) / Judge (Qwen) -> fail-loud crash on the run.
663:        # 16384 -> reasoning ~15564 + verdict room ~820 (generous for Mirror JSON + Judge enum).
664:        #
665:        # I-run11-004: the DECOMPOSITION Sentinel (MiniMax-M2, reasoning ON) emits a JSON
666:        # {verdict, unsupported_atoms, atoms} body — a multi-atom list needs MORE output room than a
667:        # bare verdict, AND the certification used reasoning + max_tokens>=3000. So the decomposition
668:        # Sentinel gets its OWN generous budget (default 16384, hard-floored at 3000 so an env
669:        # override can never re-introduce the run-12 truncation that collapses every claim to a
670:        # fail-closed UNGROUNDED). Other reasoning verifiers keep PG_VERIFIER_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS.
671:        if request.role == "sentinel":
672:            # I-arch-003 (#1253, operator "max max"): MAX output without hard-erroring. Under
673:            # allow_fallbacks:False the binding cap is the MIN max_completion_tokens across the
674:            # Sentinel provider chain (live OpenRouter read 2026-06-14): google-vertex=196608,
675:            # novita=131072, atlas-cloud=196608, minimax=131072 -> min=131072. 196608 would 400 on
676:            # the novita/minimax fallbacks, so 131072 is the safe max-of-chain (still ~8x the old
677:            # 16384 and ~40x the I-run11-004 certified 3000 floor; the multi-atom decomposition JSON
678:            # never approaches it). max_tokens is a usage-billed cap, so the generous budget never
679:            # starves output. Hard-floored at the certified minimum so an env override can't
680:            # re-introduce the run-12 truncation.
681:            decomp_budget = int(os.getenv("PG_SENTINEL_DECOMPOSITION_MAX_TOKENS", "131072"))
682:            # floor at the certified minimum, ceiling at the chain min (I-arch-003 hardening).
683:            body["max_tokens"] = min(
684:                max(decomp_budget, _SENTINEL_DECOMPOSITION_MIN_MAX_TOKENS),
685:                _SENTINEL_MAX_TOKENS_CHAIN_MIN,
686:            )
687:        elif request.role == "mirror":
688:            # I-run11-008 (#1053): BOUND GLM's reasoning. `effort` is a NO-OP on GLM (its OpenRouter
689:            # endpoints don't list it), so xhigh let thinking run unbounded and exhaust max_tokens ->
690:            # 47 empty-content blanks. Replace effort with a NUMERIC reasoning cap (GLM honors
691:            # reasoning.max_tokens) and give the verdict a generous total budget STRICTLY above the cap
692:            # (OpenRouter rule). Reasoning STAYS ON (bake-off: reasoning-off doubled false_bind), just
693:            # capped so it cannot starve the answer.
694:            #
695:            # I-arch-003 (#1253, operator "max max"): the 47-blank trap is specifically
696:            # reasoning_cap ~= max_tokens (reasoning eats the WHOLE budget -> empty content). The fix

codex
The verifier transport has better retry/backoff coverage than the section synthesis path: it has bounded transport retries, rate-limit retries, and a loop watchdog. I’m shifting attention to where verifier work is scheduled, because parallel claim verification can still overspend before the parent notices a run budget breach.

375:    # PARALLEL path (Codex iter-2 P1.1 + P1.2): enforce the run budget DURING compute, not after the
376:    # whole pool drains. Each claim is submitted with its OWN parent-captured context snapshot so the
377:    # worker inherits the Path-B capture sink/role registered on the parent. We iterate
378:    # `as_completed` and, for each completed future, re-add the worker's per-claim verifier delta to
379:    # the SINGLE parent run counter and re-check the cap — so a cumulative cap breach raises after
380:    # only ~(workers-in-flight) claims have spent, NOT all n. The pool is managed MANUALLY (not via
381:    # `with`, whose `__exit__` waits for ALL pending futures) so on a breach / worker exception we
382:    # `shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)` to cancel still-queued claims, then re-raise
383:    # (P2.2 cancel-on-fail).
384:    #
385:    # P2.1 (aborted-run cost under-accounting — documented, not over-engineered): on a worker
386:    # exception the worker's partial paid spend lives in the worker's isolated copied context and is
387:    # NOT reconciled into the parent counter. This is the SAME accepted tradeoff the seam-timeout
388:    # wrapper documents (run_honest_sweep_r3.py ~L4587-4596: in-flight verifier cost on the
389:    # held/aborted path is not reconciled) — prompt fail-closed termination outranks exact accounting
390:    # on an already-aborted run, and the operator authorized the spend.
391:    computed: list[tuple[ClaimPipelineResult, float | None] | None] = [None] * n
392:    # Codex iter-2 P1 (REGRESSION FIX): the progress write below targets `run_dir`, but

394:    # historically created `run_dir`). Existing callers (scripts/dr_benchmark/offline_e2e.py,
395:    # tests/dr_benchmark/test_offline_e2e.py) pass a `run_dir` that does NOT yet exist, so the
396:    # parallel path's progress write raised FileNotFoundError. Ensure the dir exists exactly once
397:    # at the TOP of the parallel branch (idempotent; the later kg_store reuses the same dir —
398:    # harmless). The SEQUENTIAL fast path does NOT mkdir (it writes no progress file) so it stays
399:    # byte-equivalent to the pre-#1042 behaviour.
400:    run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
401:    progress_path = run_dir / FOUR_ROLE_COMPUTE_PROGRESS_FILENAME
402:    done = 0
403:    pool = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_CLAIM_WORKERS)
404:    try:
405:        futures = [
406:            pool.submit(_compute_one, idx, claim, contextvars.copy_context())
407:            for idx, claim in enumerate(claims)
408:        ]
409:        for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures):
410:            # `future.result()` re-raises any worker exception (fail closed) — handled below.
411:            idx, result, delta = future.result()
412:            computed[idx] = (result, delta)
413:            # P2.3 + Codex iter-2 P2 (observability): write the tiny on-disk progress marker BEFORE
414:            # the budget enforcement below, so the just-completed claim — INCLUDING a cap-breaching
415:            # one — is reflected in four_role_compute_progress.json even when the very next
416:            # check_run_budget(0) raises BudgetExceededError. `done` counts completed claims
417:            # including the current one. Parent-only write — never inside a worker. A hung compute
418:            # is thus visible on disk DURING compute (the role_call_log only grows during the later,
419:            # parent-only reduction).
420:            done += 1
421:            progress_path.write_text(
422:                json.dumps({"done": done, "total": n}, sort_keys=True) + "\n",
423:                encoding="utf-8",
424:            )
425:            # Enforce the run budget DURING compute (P1.1): thread this claim's verifier spend into
426:            # the SINGLE parent run counter and re-check the cap immediately. A `BudgetExceededError`
427:            # here bounds overspend to the workers in-flight at the breach (~workers-1), not all n.
428:            if delta is not None:
429:                _add_run_cost(delta)
430:                check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if the cap is now exceeded.
431:            # I-obs-001 #1141 AC1 (Codex AC1-gate P2): forward done/total to the run-status heartbeat
432:            # AFTER _add_run_cost so running_cost_usd reflects the just-completed claim. The progress
433:            # marker above already recorded this claim BEFORE the budget check (cap-breach visibility);
434:            # if check_run_budget raised, the terminal heartbeat captures the abort. Opaque callback —
435:            # sweep_integration stays ignorant of the heartbeat schema; additive, never raises.
436:            if heartbeat_claims_cb is not None:
437:                try:
438:                    heartbeat_claims_cb(done, n)
439:                except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
440:                    pass
441:    except BaseException:
442:        # On ANY failure (BudgetExceededError from the cap OR a propagated worker exception) cancel
443:        # still-pending claims and tear the pool down NON-BLOCKING (P2.2), then re-raise so the
444:        # existing budget-abort / fail-closed path in run_four_role_evaluation handles it. No
445:        # `except: pass` — the error always propagates.
446:        pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
447:        raise
448:    else:
449:        pool.shutdown(wait=True)

451:    # Defensive: every index must be filled (a None here would mean a future silently dropped — a
452:    # real bug, never a vacuous pass). Fail loud rather than reduce a partial result set.
453:    for idx, item in enumerate(computed):
454:        if item is None:
455:            raise RuntimeError(

457:                f"compute pool (fail-closed — a dropped future must never reduce silently)."
458:            )

129:class RecordingTransport:
130:    """Wrap an injected `RoleTransport`; record EVERY completion before returning it.
131:
132:    On each `complete(request)` the wrapper appends a `RoleCallRecord(role, model_slug,
133:    served_model, raw_text, parsed=None)` to `self.records` BEFORE returning the underlying
134:    response (Codex iter-2 P1-4). Because the record is appended before the adapter sees the
135:    response — and therefore before the adapter can parse or raise — the served-identity
136:    record survives even on the fail-closed paths (a Mirror pass-1 that then raises
137:    `MirrorCitationError`). `parsed` is left None here: it is the role contract that parses,
138:    and the parsed sub-results are carried separately on `ClaimPipelineResult`.
139:
140:    I-meta-008 — RUN-BUDGET ACCOUNTING. `complete()` is the SINGLE per-call chokepoint every
141:    verifier completion (Mirror pass-1, pass-2, Sentinel, Judge) flows through exactly once.
142:    After recording, it threads the just-served call's cost into the SHARED `PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN`
143:    accumulator (`_orc._add_run_cost`) and calls `_orc.check_run_budget(0)`, which raises
144:    `BudgetExceededError` BEFORE the next paid verifier call once the generator + verifier total
145:    crosses the cap. The transport itself adds no paid call here — it ACCOUNTS for the
146:    already-served call.
147:    """
148:
149:    def __init__(self, transport: RoleTransport) -> None:
150:        self._transport = transport
151:        self.records: list[RoleCallRecord] = []
152:
153:    def complete(self, request: RoleRequest) -> RoleResponse:
154:        response = self._transport.complete(request)
155:        # Append BEFORE returning so a downstream adapter raise cannot drop the record. The
156:        # served-identity audit trail for the breaching call is preserved even when the budget
157:        # check below raises.
158:        self.records.append(
159:            RoleCallRecord(
160:                role=request.role,
161:                model_slug=request.model_slug,
162:                served_model=response.served_model,
163:                raw_text=response.raw_text,
164:                parsed=None,
165:                # I-meta-002-q1b (#939): carry the separated reasoning so the seam can persist it
166:                # to four_role_role_calls.jsonl apart from the verdict (None if the role had none).
167:                reasoning=response.reasoning,
168:            )
169:        )
170:        # I-meta-008: account THIS verifier call's cost into the SAME run-budget accumulator the
171:        # generator feeds, then enforce the cap. `_RUN_COST_CTX` is per-asyncio-Task; the seam
172:        # runs synchronously inside the same `run_one_query` task that holds the generator spend,
173:        # so the delta lands on the counter holding `generator_spend + verifier_spend`.
174:        _role_cost = compute_role_call_cost(request.model_slug, response.usage)
175:        _orc._add_run_cost(_role_cost)
176:        # FX-11 (I-ready-017 BUG-10b + Codex iter-1 P1): the four-role verifier calls
177:        # (mirror/sentinel/judge) wrote ZERO cost-ledger rows before this. Ledger THIS call through
178:        # the SINGLE canonical writer, which bumps the shared per-session accumulator and appends
179:        # the row atomically — so role-row cumulative is non-monotonic NEITHER across the parallel
180:        # claim workers (each reset their own _RUN_COST_CTX) NOR in file write order. Best-effort
181:        # inside the writer: a ledger failure never breaks the verifier call or the budget check.
182:        _ub = response.usage or {}
183:        _rt = int(_ub.get("reasoning_tokens", 0) or 0) or int(
184:            (_ub.get("completion_tokens_details") or {}).get("reasoning_tokens", 0) or 0
185:        )
186:        _orc.append_cost_ledger_row(
187:            session_id=_orc._CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX.get() or "no_run_id",
188:            call_type=f"role:{request.role}",
189:            cost_usd=_role_cost,

480:    `run_dir`, so question N can reuse questions 1..N-1's VERIFIED claims. Default None preserves the

codex
The 4-role claim pool enforces budget only as completed futures return. That is better than waiting for the whole pool, but it still lets several in-flight claims spend past the cap before the parent can cancel; I’ll record that as a cost-cap overshoot risk, not as a release/faithfulness failure.

233:    """COMPUTE the per-claim 4-role pipeline for every claim, returning results BY INPUT INDEX.
234:
235:    I-run11-001 (#1042), Codex Path-B SAFE design (.codex/I-run11-seam). This is the ONLY half
236:    that runs the (already-paid) verifier calls; it makes NO reduction decision — the parent
237:    reduces in input order. Each element is `(ClaimPipelineResult, cost_delta)` for `claims[idx]`,
238:    where:
239:
240:      * SEQUENTIAL (`_CLAIM_WORKERS == 1` or a single claim): `run_claim_pipeline` is called
241:        DIRECTLY on the parent thread (no copied context, no `reset_run_cost`), so
242:        `RecordingTransport.complete()` enforces the LIVE per-call run budget on the parent
243:        `_RUN_COST_CTX` EXACTLY as today. `cost_delta is None` signals the parent "already
244:        accounted — do not re-add" (byte-equivalence with the pre-#1042 behaviour).
245:
246:      * PARALLEL: each claim runs inside its OWN `contextvars.copy_context()` snapshot that is taken
247:        ON THE PARENT THREAD BEFORE submit (Codex iter-2 P1.2 — copying inside the worker would
248:        snapshot the worker's EMPTY default context, so the parent Path-B capture sink
249:        `pathB_capture._SINK` / `_ROLE` registered by `pathB_runner` on the PARENT would be ABSENT
250:        and verifier capture would no-op, failing post-run completeness when workers>1). Each claim
251:        gets its OWN copy (never one shared copy across concurrent workers — a copy carries the
252:        worker's isolated `_RUN_COST_CTX` AND the parent's `_SINK` reference). The worker
253:        `reset_run_cost()`s its copy, runs the pipeline, and returns `current_run_cost()` as
254:        `cost_delta`; the PARENT re-adds it to the single run counter and enforces the cap DURING
255:        compute (Codex iter-2 P1.1) so overspend is bounded to the workers in-flight at breach
256:        (~workers-1), not all claims. The pathB capture `_SINK` is shared BY REFERENCE through the
257:        copied context (atomic `list.append`), so verifier captures land at the PARENT sink for the
258:        M4 gate — it is NOT isolated. A worker exception PROPAGATES (fail closed; no `except: pass`).

262:    # I-ready-017 FX-08b (#1113): CLAIM-LEVEL DEDUP (BUG-04 determinism half).

264:    # (a non-deterministic release gate). Two claims whose ENTIRE pipeline INPUT
265:    # is identical MUST get the same verdict — so run the pipeline ONCE per
266:    # distinct input and fan the verdict out. The key is the FULL input the
267:    # pipeline consumes (claim_text + evidence doc_id+text + severity +
268:    # s0_categories), NOT just (sentence+ids+spans): anything that could
269:    # legitimately change the verdict is in the key, so two genuinely-different
270:    # claims can NEVER collapse to one verdict (faithfulness-safe; identical
271:    # input => identical output is the only justification for sharing a verdict).
272:    # The evidence doc `text` is the FX-03 cited WINDOW, so it encodes the span.

279:    # the per-claim run. Recursion on representatives is one level deep (reps have

283:    def _dedup_key(c: FourRoleClaim) -> tuple:
284:        return (
285:            " ".join((c.claim_text or "").split()),
286:            tuple(sorted(
287:                (d.doc_id, d.text) for d in (c.evidence_documents or [])
288:            )),
289:            c.severity,
290:            tuple(sorted(c.s0_categories or [])),
291:        )

293:    if _dedup_enabled and n > 1:
294:        rep_idx_by_key: dict[tuple, int] = {}
295:        rep_indices: list[int] = []
296:        for idx, claim in enumerate(claims):
297:            k = _dedup_key(claim)
298:            if k not in rep_idx_by_key:
299:                rep_idx_by_key[k] = idx
300:                rep_indices.append(idx)
301:        if len(rep_indices) < n:
302:            # At least one duplicate — run the pipeline only on representatives.
303:            rep_results = _compute_claim_results(

331:    ) -> tuple[int, ClaimPipelineResult, float]:

337:            # at the parent sink.

347:                timestamp=timestamp,
348:            )

353:        result, delta = worker_ctx.run(_run)
